LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS DEFENDANT JAMES LIU'S MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT. RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: GRANTED. THE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR MALICIOUS PROSECUTION IMPLICATES CCP 425.16 [ANTI-SLAPP STATUTE]. SEE DANIELS V. ROBBINS (2010) 182 CAL.APP.4TH 204, 215 ("THE PLAIN LANGUAGE OF THE ANTI-SLAPP STATUTE DICTATES THAT EVERY CLAIM OF MALICIOUS PROSECUTION IS A CAUSE OF ACTION ARISING FROM PROTECTED ACTIVITY BECAUSE EVERY SUCH CLAIM NECESSARILY DEPENDS UPON WRITTEN AND ORAL STATEMENTS IN A PRIOR JUDICIAL PROCEEDING.") THE OPPOSING PARTY (MR. YORK LIU) FAILS TO DEMONSTRATE A LIKELIHOOD OF PREVAILING ON THE MERITS. MR. JAMES LIU'S "REQUEST FOR DOMESTIC VIOLENCE RESTRAINING ORDER" WAS A PURE FAMILY LAW MATTER AND IT CANNOT BE THE BASIS FOR A MALICIOUS PROSECUTION ACTION BASED ON THE PUBLIC POLICIES HIGHLIGHTED IN BIDNA V. ROSEN (1993) 19 CAL.APP.4TH 27 AND NICHOLSON V. FAZELI (2003) 113 CAL.APP.4TH 1091. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR5976

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-22-2014 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Ross M. Madden, Esq. of the Law Office of Ross Madden, Tel. 415.567.1212, for Cross Complainant and opposing party;
No other appearance.

RULING: The Court adopted the tentative ruling which was not contested. CROSS DEFENDANT JAMES LIU'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT is overruled. A cross-complaint that is subject-matter related to the plaintiff's complaint relates back to when the action was commenced for statute of limitations purposes. (Sidney v. Sup. Ct. (1988) 198 Cal.App.3d 710, 714.) The First Amended Cross Complaint ("FACC") alleges that the injury occurred on December 24, 2010. The underlying complaint was filed on December 24, 2012. The three year statute of limitations provided by CCP sec. 340.5 had not yet expired at the time of the filing of the underlying complaint and the FACC is therefore timely. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: A. James Robertson II; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR# 5976.

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT

MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-26-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Simone McCormick, Esq., 415.788.1900, for Plaintiff/Cross Defendant James Liu MD.
Dane Jones, Esq., 415.438.5961, specially appearing for Plaintiff/Cross Defendant James Liu MD.
Ross Madden, Esq., 415.567.1212, for Defendant/Cross Complainant York Liu.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: CROSS DEFENDANT JAMES LIU'S MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT is granted. The cause of action for malicious prosecution implicates CCP 425.16 [anti-SLAPP statute]. See Daniels v. Robbins (2010) 182 Cal.App.4th 204, 215 ("The plain language of the anti-SLAPP statute dictates that every claim of malicious prosecution is a cause of action arising from protected activity because every such claim necessarily depends upon written and oral statements in a prior judicial proceeding.") The opposing party (Mr. York Liu) fails to demonstrate a likelihood of prevailing on the merits. Mr. James Liu's "Request for Domestic Violence Restraining Order" was a pure family law matter and it cannot be the basis for a malicious prosecution action based on the public policies highlighted in Bidna v. Rosen (1993) 19 Cal.App.4th 27 and Nicholson v. Fazeli (2003) 113 Cal.App.4th 1091. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR5976
